extraction.one_line_summary: HODEG Alvaro won a flat sprint after Quickstep-Floors deployed their lead-out train with 3km to go, with MØRKØV delivering him at 800m out
tactical_patterns: Quickstep-Floors started their lead-out at 3km (unusually early), banking on train durability over positioning surprise tactics
JUNGELS worked until 800m as a super-domestique, revealing Quickstep's willingness to burn GC options for sprint stages
BONIFACIO attempted solo leadout for Bahrain-Merida, suggesting team lacked depth for organized train against Quickstep firepower

rider_signals.HODEG Alvaro: positive: 21-year-old confidently took stage after recent Handzame Classic win, showing repeatable form transfer across race types
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: negative: Had designated leadout (MCCARTHY) but couldn't match Hodeg's explosive speed despite positioning
rider_signals.JUNGELS Bob: neutral: Luxembourg road champion worked deep into finale (800m) as super-domestique, sacrificing GC positioning early in stage race
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: negative: Observed freewheeling in peloton rather than positioning up front, showing lack of motivation for sprint stages
